title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping · Career Campaign Defense
summary: A career adviser, working practitioner, and accessibility-aware peer reviewer. They need a learner-owned search charter, source-dated market hypothesis, decomposed role, honest evidence matrix, explicit constraints, and a defensible apply or investigate decision. Planned artifact: Deliver Target Role and Evidence Strategy Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Success Definition
steps.instruction:
project case — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The project variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Success Definition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date. For Commit to the mission model · Project Success Definition, keep Success Definition connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

steps.why: Retrieving Go No Go through the concrete role requirement and evidence matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Go No Go, keep Go No Go connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

hints: For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Go No Go, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Go No Go precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Go No Go, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The project variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Go No Go, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission.
